skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound.
skill.summary: Create AI-generated videos using 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance, and Wan through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.author: tool-belt

security_audit.summary: All 66 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code fences (triple-backtick blocks) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. Hardcoded URLs are legitimate documentation links and example placeholders. The two high-severity weak cryptography findings are false positives with no cryptographic content present at the reported locations. The skill legitimately requires external command execution (infsh CLI) and network access (inference.sh API), which are documented risk factors but present no security concern for a CLI-based video generation tool. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

content.user_title: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance and 40+ models
content.value_statement: Creating professional video content typically requires expensive software and production teams. This skill lets you generate videos from text prompts or images using 40+ AI models through the inference.sh CLI, including Google Veo 3.1, Seedance 1.5 Pro, and Wan 2.5.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and other models
Animate still images into videos with image-to-video models like Wan 2.5
Create AI avatar videos with lipsync using OmniHuman, Fabric and PixVerse
Add sound effects (foley) to silent videos with HunyuanVideo Foley
Upscale video resolution with Topaz Video Upscaler
Merge multiple video clips with transitions using Media Merger
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and authenticated before use
Video generation requires an active inference.sh account with available credits
Processing times vary by model and video length, some models take several minutes
Not all models support all features such as audio generation or high resolution output

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate engaging video content for social media platforms from text descriptions without filming equipment or editing skills.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Marketing and Product Demos
use_cases.description: Create product demonstration videos and marketing content using text-to-video and avatar animation capabilities.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing professionals and small business owners

use_cases.title: Educational and Training Videos
use_cases.description: Produce explainer videos and training materials with AI avatars and lipsync for internal or public educational content.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short video showing a timelapse of a sunset over the ocean, suitable for a relaxation video.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: First time generating AI video

prompt_templates.title: Animate a product image
prompt_templates.prompt: Take this product photo and create a short video showing it rotating slowly with professional lighting, suitable for an e-commerce listing.

prompt_templates.title: Create an AI avatar presentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an avatar video using this portrait image and narration audio, showing a professional presenter speaking to camera for a training module.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Avatar animation with lipsync for training content

prompt_templates.title: Build a complete video workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a text-to-video clip with Veo, add foley sound effects with HunyuanVideo, upscale the result with Topaz, and merge it with an intro clip using Media Merger.

output_examples.input: Generate a video of a drone shot flying over a forest at golden hour.
output: Executing: infsh app run google/veo-3-1-fast with prompt
Video generated successfully at 1080p resolution, 8 seconds duration
Video URL returned from inference.sh platform ready for download

output_examples.input: Animate this product image with a slow rotation effect.
output: Executing: infsh app run falai/wan-2-5 with image_url parameter
Image-to-video processing complete, 5 second video at 720p
Animated video file available via inference.sh download link

best_practices: Always run infsh login before first use to authenticate your inference.sh account and verify CLI access.
Start with shorter duration and lower resolution settings to test prompts before generating full quality videos.
Use image-to-video models when you have a specific visual starting point, as they provide more controlled results than text-only prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not generate videos without first testing prompts at lower quality settings to conserve credits and reduce wait times.
Avoid using placeholder URLs from examples in production requests, replace them with your actual image and video URLs.
Do not assume all models support the same input parameters, check the specific model documentation for available options before running.

faq.question: What do I need to install before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the inference.sh CLI (infsh) installed on your system and an active inference.sh account. Run infsh login to authenticate before using any video generation commands.

faq.question: Which model should I use for the best video quality?
faq.answer: Google Veo 3.1 provides the highest quality output. For faster generation use Veo 3.1 Fast. For budget-friendly options use Pruna models like P-Video or WAN-T2V.

faq.question: Can I add audio to generated videos?
faq.answer: Some models like Veo 3.1 Fast and Veo 3 support optional audio generation. You can also add separate foley sound effects using the HunyuanVideo Foley utility after generation.

faq.question: How do I create a talking avatar video?
faq.answer: Use an avatar model like OmniHuman 1.5, Fabric 1.0, or PixVerse Lipsync. Provide a portrait image and an audio file, then run the model via infsh app run with both URLs as input.

faq.question: How can I browse all available video models?
faq.answer: Run infsh app list --category video in your terminal to see all 250+ video models available on the inference.sh platform.

faq.question: Is there a cost associated with generating videos?
faq.answer: Yes, video generation uses inference.sh platform credits. Costs vary by model and video parameters. Check your inference.sh account dashboard for current pricing and available credits.
